pi_items.source: Explicit statement in task.json description: 'You must create and apply at least 3 different tags total across your library items'. Also confirmed in README.md: 'Add at least 3 different tags to items in your library'.

Task description uses plural 'items' ('applied them to items') implying multiple items should be tagged, but does not explicitly specify a minimum of 2. The requirement for 2 items is configured in task.json metadata and used by verifier.py but is not explicitly stated in the task description or README.
File located at benchmarks/cua_world/environments/zotero_env/assets/sample_data/machine_learning_papers.ris. Examined file content shows standard RIS format with TY, AU, TI, JO, VL, etc. fields.

Counted entries in machine_learning_papers.ris file. Contains papers by: LeCun (Deep Learning), Krizhevsky (ImageNet), Goodfellow (GANs), Vaswani (Attention is All You Need), Silver (AlphaGo), Brown (GPT-3/Language Models), Devlin (BERT), He (ResNet).
README.md line 11 states: 'This environment provides a fully functional Zotero 7.0.11 installation on Ubuntu 22.04'
Confirmed in README.md line 14, setup_task.sh line 5, export_result.sh line 11, and seed_library.py line 44. All scripts reference this SQLite database path.

README.md line 63 states: 'These files contain real academic papers from published literature, providing authentic bibliographic metadata.' All papers in machine_learning_papers.ris are real published papers (e.g., LeCun et al. 2015 Nature 521:436-444, Krizhevsky et al. NIPS 2012, Vaswani et al. 2017, etc.)

pi_items.verified_value: Tags must be >2 characters, not generic patterns (tag1, test, etc.), and contain letters
pi_items.source: verifier.py lines 68-89 define lazy tag patterns and quality criteria: >2 chars, no lazy patterns, contains letters. Tags like 'deep-learning', 'neural-networks', 'computer-vision', 'NLP' all meet these requirements.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires adding at least 3 different tags to items in a Zotero 7.0.11 library (verified). Tags should be applied to items and must be meaningful (>2 chars, containing letters, not generic like 'tag1'). The verifier expects a minimum of 2 items to be tagged, though this is not explicitly stated in the task description. The sample data contains 8 real machine learning papers from published literature (LeCun Deep Learning 2015, Krizhevsky ImageNet 2012, Vaswani Attention 2017, etc.) in RIS format. The Zotero database uses SQLite at /home/ga/Zotero/zotero.sqlite with itemTags table for tag-item relationships.
